title: Welcome to Addis: A Relocation Guide for Expats and Diplomats.
excerpt: Expats and diplomats: documents, housing visits, security culture, timing, shipping choices, movers, schools.
content:
Welcome to Addis: a relocation guide for expats and diplomats

Arriving in Addis Ababa as an expat or diplomat means layering household logistics onto visas, housing searches, and compound rules. This guide frames what to sequence first: documentation copies, realistic move-day windows, security culture at gates, and how to brief local movers so your first weeks feel structured—not improvised.

Before furniture moves: paperwork rhythm

Keep passport copies, lease letters, and employer introduction notes in both cloud and paper folders. Gates and compounds often prefer printed manifests with vehicle plates spelled exactly as registered.

Housing visits that actually inform moving

Walk evenings and weekends before signing. Measure door diagonals and elevator interiors if you import oversized pieces—shipping containers forgive nothing that stairwells refuse.

Security culture at compounds

Low voices, no casual photography toward sensitive sightlines, and patience at checkpoints clear faster than debate. Diplomatic-adjacent areas may add COI or additional insured lines—submit early.

Choosing arrival timing

Align truck arrival with gate officer hours and freight elevator bookings. Friday afternoon optimism collides with traffic and prayer windows—mid-week starts often breathe easier.

What to ship versus buy locally

  • Voltage-sensitive electronics need transformer plans.
  • Bulky furniture may cost more to ship than replace—run cube math.
  • Sentimental and document boxes should travel with you, not loose in sea freight.

School runs and commute truth

Test morning drives to embassies, schools, and offices. Distance on maps understates certain nodes during peak—your mover needs realistic ETAs, not bravado minutes.

Health and jet lag buffers

Schedule heavy deliveries after sleep debt eases. Exhausted decisions scratch walls and fray tempers—pad your first week.

Cultural courtesy that speeds logistics

Tipping optional but appreciated; coffee offers build goodwill with guards and neighbors. Movers who model calm at barriers protect your schedule.

Choosing movers who speak expat workflows

Ask for compound references, English or French briefings if needed, and WhatsApp supervisor chains. Experience with diplomatic pockets matters.

Currency and cash buffers

Keep small bills for tips and incidental gate fees—card hiccups on arrival week are normal stress multipliers.

Medical and pharmacy first week

Locate clinics your embassy recommends; pack prescription copies in carry-on—do not bury them in sea freight.

Driver and helper etiquette

Clear briefings on addresses in English and Amharic when possible reduce wrong turns that exhaust everyone.

Conclusion

Welcome to Addis works when paperwork, timing, and security respect ride alongside sofas. Plan gates and elevators before containers land—your first impression of home should be orderly, not chaotic.
author: Active Movers Team
